qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Aliased PRIVATE fields are cached in the public response cache and leak cross-user data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a race in @envelop/graphql-modules where useGraphQLModules(application) mixes @ExecutionContext() values across concurrent requests. This finding instead targets alias handling in @envelop/response-cache's PRIVATE-cache gate and shared cache storage, so it does not identify the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's useGraphQLModules context race or the concurrent-request cross-contamination of @ExecutionContext()-injected singleton services.
evidence: GHSA-h3hw-29fv-2x75 describes a race condition in @envelop/graphql-modules where useGraphQLModules(application) bypasses the async context fix and mixes request context values between parallel requests.

The only final qualifying finding, response-cache-private-scope-alias-bypass, concerns aliased PRIVATE fields being misclassified in @envelop/response-cache and then stored in a shared response cache.
The qualifying finding's code locations, exploit path, and PoC evidence do not involve useGraphQLModules, graphql-modules service injection, @ExecutionContext(), or concurrent request state bleed.
